[PDF_PAGE 306 | score=49] , Organ. His design is unique enough to warrant a separate entry> He designed the organ with 7 usable stops and 6S working keys ( 15 black keys and 50 while keys). For the stops he used a simple binary code. Each stop represents a number, the number of possible combinations for stop positioning is 128. This range is from zero to 127. See the example given below I • the stop is in 0 .. the SlOp is out 64 32 16 8 o 0 0 0 o 0 0 0 o I I [ J 0 0 0 r r I • o o o o I 2 o o o o I I 0 - 0 I - I 1- 57 1- 65 1- 127 Once the stops have been set, find out what the results are. If the player sets the stops at: I 0 0 0 100, the result is 68. Referring to the stop result table indicates thaI 68 is a random 
[PDF_PAGE 35 | score=44] ,St1I.ff of Hanning of Harming XPValu e: 4,500 G PValue: 35,000 Red Arrow, Black Shield The staff of harming functions like a reversed staff of healing , but at the cost of one charge per creature harmed . It inflicts Id6+ I points of dam­ age if iltouches any creature (no saving throw); a normal attack roll may be required. This is in addition to norma! weapon damages (ld6 points), if applicable. The staff can also create the follow­ ing effects, with the costs noted. Each effect is identical to the reversed form of a priest spell. Note that the use of this staff is an evil act. Eff«:t Cause blindness Cause disease Cause serious wounds Create poison of Healing Charg es 2 charges 2 charges ]
[PDF_PAGE 43 | score=39] ... slight mishap in the creation of the device: the steps are not followed in the proper order , the astrolog ical signs do not bode well, the item 's creator is disturbed to begin with, the instructions are incomplete, or something just plain goes wrong. Any of these results may cause a magical item to behave differently from others just like it. Not all quirks are bad, however. Some are detrimental to the item or its user, some are neu­ tral, and some are even beneficia l. The possibili­ ties of quirks existing (and the actual number of quirks) are determined by using the table below to determine lhe exact quirks a magical staff possesses. Roll 01-86 87- 94 95- 98 99-00 Roll 01-04 05--07 
[PDF_PAGE 32 | score=36] Each staff contains the following powers when used on the Prime Material Plane: • A +4 bonus to saving throws vs, attack forms based on that element. • Complete immunity to attacks by any elemen­ tal of that type. • The ability to summon one 8 HD elemental of that type per day (as the wizard spell), each sum­ moning costing one charge. • Certain spel1-like effects , each costing one charge per use. These created spell effects are treated as if cast by a 10th-level spel1caster. The effects are dependent on the type of element as follows: Earth; web, wall of stone Air: lightning bolt, cloudkill Fire:fireball, wall offire Waler : ice storm or wall When used on the elemental plane of the corre­ 
[PDF_PAGE 11 | score=34] ,Spellbook, Unique Mageries Spelltrap (Abjuration! Alteration) Le\'el: 7 Component s: V, S, M Range: Special Casting Time: 7 Duration: I round per level Saving T hrow: None Area of Effect: Special Definition: When this spell is cast, the wiza rd causes an oval , glowing ball of silvery hue to form in the air over (I to 2 feet above) his or her head. It floats there si lently unlil the spell expires, and need not be further concentrated upon to maintain ils existence. Any spell, magi­ cal power , or magical item effect that is cast directly upon the w izard (as opposed to area­ effect magic) will be attracted to, and utterly absorbed by, this floating spell/rap . This includes magic missiles 
[PDF_PAGE 45 | score=34] , Staff. Rollalan on the siafl The slaffbearer can clearly see magi­ cal auras, but Ihe staff does not enable others not touching it 10 do so, No charges are drained, The bearer can also excrc ise telekinesis on any object touched by the staff. This function must be exercised as the touch is made , is activated and ende d by silen t force of will, and drains three charges, plus one charge per round after the round of activation. Moving creatures and objects require a success ful attack roll to be touc hed, and any living, conscio us, unwilling creature receives a saving throw vs. spell each round to break free of the power. Telekinesis is identi ca l in effect to the 5th-level wizard's spell
[PDF_PAGE 236 | score=34] . - "' initiative modifi er is +2, and the effect lasts just one second. The temperature is - I OO(DG) F and damage is 6d6, treating all 1 s rolled as 25 (6<16, 12-36). The cost is two charges per use. A saving throw vs. wand is applicable. Gre mlin XP Value: 3,000 G P Value: 10,000 Top Ballisl& This wand is often a stout shaft of mahogany or ebony some 18 inches long, lipped with a ..... 'OOden claw into which is set a sphere o f quartz. It has three uses. First, the wand can detect gremlins. It does this auto matically within 120 feet (ifthcrc is more than one g remlin in range , it det ects the nearest). Withi n the sphere. a sma ll blue glow shows the directio n of the gremlin, and the c
[PDF_PAGE 53 | score=33] occurred, the staff was useful to certain powerful wizards guarding the borders of Nyrond. Possession of this wooden staff allows a wizard of at least II th level to attract personal followers as a fighter of the same level. A wiza rd can use the stafT only once per year, and it must remain in the spellcaster's possession for a full month after the followers have gathered, or the troops will disband and desert. Once the month has passed, the troops will be personally loyal to the spell­ caster for one year, barrin g exceptional cond i­ tions or poor treatment . Note that this in no way affects the hirin g of men-at-arms in the norma l fashion. of the Whit e Well XP Value: 15,000 CP Value: 75
